title: Bookmap Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees

content:
Table of Contents

  • Bookmap Overview (2026)
  • Bookmap Pricing Guide 2026: Plans & Costs
  • Hidden Fees & Additional Costs
  • Quick Comparison: Bookmap vs Alternatives
  • Who Should Use Bookmap?
  • Bookmap Pricing FAQ (2026)
  • Final Recommendation: Is Bookmap Worth It in 2026?

Bookmap Overview (2026)

Bookmap is a specialized order flow and liquidity visualization platform, rated 4.4/5 by TradingToolsHub users. Unlike traditional charting tools, Bookmap offers a unique heatmap interface that reveals real-time market microstructure, making it a favorite among futures scalpers, day traders, and crypto traders who want to see institutional activity, spoofing, and absorption in action. For a deep dive into its features and user experience, see our Bookmap review.

Bookmap stands out for its:

  • Unmatched order flow visualization (heatmap, DOM, liquidity tracking)
  • Free crypto tier with live data
  • Advanced indicators: Large Lot Tracker, Iceberg Detector, CVD, Strength Level, Stops & Icebergs Map
  • Focus on market microstructure over traditional technical analysis

However, Bookmap is not a one-stop charting solution. It lacks classic indicators (RSI, MACD, candlesticks), so many users pair it with another platform. For a side-by-side breakdown, check our Bookmap vs eSignal comparison.

Bookmap Pricing Guide 2026: Plans & Costs

Bookmap offers several pricing tiers in 2026, ranging from a robust free plan for crypto traders to premium subscriptions for futures and stocks. Here’s a detailed breakdown:

Plan Monthly Price Annual Price Markets Supported Key Features
Digital (Free) $0 $0 Crypto Live crypto heatmap, basic indicators
Digital Plus $19 $180 Crypto Advanced indicators, more historical data
Global $69 $660 Futures, Crypto Full order flow suite, DOM, 1 connection
Global+ $99 $960 Futures, Crypto, Stocks All indicators, multiple connections, API access

Note: Prices are current as of June 2026. Annual plans offer a ~20% discount over monthly rates.

All paid plans come with a 14-day money-back guarantee. There is no free trial for the Global/Global+ plans, but you can use the Digital (crypto) tier for free indefinitely.

Hidden Fees & Additional Costs

Bookmap’s pricing is transparent, but there are a few extra costs to be aware of before you subscribe:

  • Market Data Fees: For futures and stocks, you’ll need to pay for real-time data from exchanges (e.g., CME, NYSE). These fees are not included in your Bookmap subscription and typically range from $5 to $120/month per exchange, depending on your broker and data package.
  • Broker/Platform Connection Fees: Some brokers or data providers may charge connection or routing fees to use Bookmap with their services.
  • Third-Party Add-Ons: Advanced indicators, custom modules, or plugins from Bookmap Marketplace may require additional one-time or subscription fees.
  • Hardware Requirements: Bookmap is resource-intensive. For optimal performance, a modern multi-core CPU and at least 16GB RAM are recommended.

For a full breakdown of what’s included and what’s extra, see our Bookmap review.

Quick Comparison: Bookmap vs Alternatives

Platform Rating Lowest Price Order Flow Tools Traditional Charts Free Plan
Bookmap 4.4/5 $0 (crypto) Best-in-class (heatmap, DOM, CVD, Iceberg) No Yes (crypto only)
eSignal 4.2/5 $56/mo Basic (Time & Sales, Level 2) Yes No
Koyfin Pro 4.1/5 $15/mo No Yes Yes (limited)
MotiveWave 4.3/5 $24/mo Good (volume profile, footprint) Yes No

For a more detailed comparison, see our Bookmap vs MotiveWave and Bookmap vs Koyfin Pro pages.

Who Should Use Bookmap?

Bookmap is purpose-built for traders who want to see what’s happening inside the market, not just on the surface. It’s ideal for:

  • Futures scalpers and day traders who rely on the DOM, order flow, and liquidity shifts
  • Crypto traders seeking institutional-grade order flow tools for free
  • Prop firm traders who need an edge in reading market microstructure
  • Traders who already use a traditional charting platform and want to add order flow visualization

If you’re looking for classic charting, indicators, or portfolio analytics, Bookmap is not a standalone solution. Pair it with a platform like Koyfin Pro or MotiveWave for a complete toolkit.

Bookmap Pricing FAQ (2026)

  • Is there a free version of Bookmap in 2026? Yes, the Digital plan is free for crypto traders with live data and basic features.
  • How much does Bookmap cost for futures trading? $69/month for Global, $99/month for Global+ (annual discounts available).
  • Are market data fees included? No, you must pay exchange fees separately for futures and stocks.
  • Can I try Bookmap before paying? Only the crypto Digital plan is free. No free trial for Global/Global+.
  • What payment methods are accepted? Credit/debit cards and PayPal are supported.

Final Recommendation: Is Bookmap Worth It in 2026?

If you’re a serious futures or crypto trader who wants to read the market’s true intent, Bookmap remains the gold standard for order flow visualization in 2026. The free crypto tier is unmatched for new traders, while the Global/Global+ plans justify their premium price with unique tools you won’t find elsewhere. Just remember to budget for exchange data fees and consider pairing Bookmap with a traditional charting platform for a complete workflow.

Still unsure? Read our full Bookmap review or compare it directly to other platforms on our comparison page.
